subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: The cumulative distribution function for health care costs experienced by a policyholder is modeled by the function $F(x) = \begin{cases} 1-e^{-x/100} & \text{for } x > 0 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases}$ The policy has a deductible of 20. An insurer reimburses the policyholder for 100% of health care costs between 20 and 120 less the deductible. Health care costs above 120 are reimbursed at 50%. Let G be the cumulative distribution function of reimbursements given that the reimbursement is positive. Calculate $G(115)$.
